behaviors.description: Primary translator output is grammatically coherent and preserves source meaning (not literal word substitution).
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is model-output quality: correctness depends on what the real neural MT model actually generates (coherence/meaning preservation), which a stub would hide. This is not Workflow because the behavior is a single model judgment, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service against the real translation model on a multilingual evaluation set and score adequacy/fluency (human rubric or semantic-quality metric thresholds) to detect meaning drift and literal-but-incorrect translations.

behaviors.description: On translation API 503, service transparently falls back to dictionary translator and still returns success.
behaviors.justification: The failure is module handoff/orchestration (API client error -> fallback translator -> caller response). Real network/OS behavior is not required to reproduce this specific failure; an in-process API stub returning 503 is sufficient. Rejected System: behavior text is about fallback logic, not transport-level timeout/socket behavior. Rejected Agentic: no assertion depends on model output quality, only control flow on error.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service + API client adapter + SQLite-backed fallback translator; make API adapter return 503; assert caller receives fallback translation (no error) and fallback path is recorded.

behaviors.description: Dictionary fallback returns correct pre-stored translation for phrases present in SQLite.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on SQLite data access and mapping from stored phrase->translation, which is a module + local DB boundary. Rejected Unit: pure function tests miss SQL/query/schema wiring failures. Rejected System: no real external network/subprocess/OS primitive beyond local DB wiring is required. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no model decision involved.
behaviors.test_description: Populate a test SQLite DB with known phrase pairs; call fallback translator through repository/service layers; assert exact translation match for existing phrases.

behaviors.description: Quality tracking computes character-level edit distance between primary and fallback outputs and stores the metric.
behaviors.justification: This behavior combines algorithm result plus persistence side effect; real failures often occur at the compute->storage handoff (wrong field, transaction, serialization). Rejected Agentic: metric computation here is deterministic string processing, not model-quality judgment. Rejected System: no real external infra is required beyond local module wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke quality-tracking path with fixed primary/fallback strings, assert stored metric equals expected edit distance in the metrics store, and verify record persistence schema/fields.

behaviors.description: When both translators are available, service returns primary translation and logs quality comparison asynchronously in background.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is orchestration and async side effect: preference policy (primary wins) plus background metric logging across local modules. Rejected Agentic: assertion does not depend on model output quality, only source selection and logging side effect. Rejected Workflow: only one model decision is relevant; no multi-agentic chain. System is not required unless implementation specifically relies on OS/subprocess primitives not stated in behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run service with both translators enabled (primary stub success + fallback deterministic lookup), assert response uses primary output immediately, then await background job completion and assert quality-comparison log/metric exists.
